assertions.expectation: Explains the key difference: surge upgrades nodes in-place with rolling replacement, blue-green creates an entirely new pool
assertions.reason: The response clearly explains that surge upgrades drain nodes while blue-green creates a new pool (green) while keeping the old pool (blue) online, then migrates workloads between pools

assertions.expectation: Recommends blue-green for this scenario due to local SSDs (data can't migrate between nodes)
assertions.reason: Explicitly recommends blue-green and states the critical issue that local SSD data can't migrate between nodes

assertions.expectation: Explains that local SSD data is lost when a node is drained during surge upgrade
assertions.reason: Clearly states 'Surge upgrades drain nodes, which destroys local SSD data' and explains the consequences for Cassandra

assertions.expectation: Recommends GKE's autoscaled blue-green upgrade strategy configured with --enable-autoscaling and --autoscaled-rollout-policy (NOT surge parameters like maxSurge/maxUnavailable which don't apply to blue-green), or describes the blue-green process if manual workflow is needed
assertions.reason: The gcloud command uses --strategy=BLUE_GREEN with --standard-rollout-policy instead of --autoscaled-rollout-policy, and doesn't include --enable-autoscaling. Uses surge-like parameters (batch-node-count, batch-soak-duration) instead of proper autoscaled blue-green parameters

assertions.expectation: Addresses Cassandra-specific concerns: need to decommission from the ring before draining, rebuild on new nodes
assertions.reason: Discusses Cassandra ring health, rebalancing, and includes monitoring commands for ring status. Also mentions decommission process in the manual alternative section

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for creating the new node pool with matching configuration
assertions.reason: Provides gcloud container node-pools update command for blue-green strategy and also shows manual node pool creation command as an alternative

assertions.expectation: Includes kubectl commands for cordoning and draining
assertions.reason: While the response mentions that nodes will be cordoned and drained, it doesn't provide the actual kubectl cordon and kubectl drain commands - it only shows monitoring commands

assertions.expectation: Mentions PDB configuration to protect the Cassandra quorum during migration
assertions.reason: Provides a complete PodDisruptionBudget YAML configuration with minAvailable: 2 to protect quorum during the upgrade process
